title: Power inverter with dynamic current limiting
abstract: A power inverter with dynamic current limiting proposes to take into consideration the actual or approximated cooling capacity of the cooling baseplate to dynamically determine the instantaneous available current that can be supplied by the inverter power circuit to a load.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A power inverter including an inverter power circuit having a DC input connectable to a power source and an AC output connectable to a load, a cooling baseplate so configured and positioned as to cool the inverter power circuit and a controller connected to the inverter power circuit; the controller being so configured as to dynamically determine the maximal available current that can be supplied by the inverter power circuit to the load as a function of a cooling capacity of the cooling baseplate. 2. The power inverter as recited in claim 1 , wherein the cooling baseplate is cooled by a cooling fluid flowing therethrough. 3. The power inverter as recited in claim 2, further including a temperature sensor to measure the temperature of the cooling fluid flowing through the cooling baseplate. 4. The power inverter as recited in claim 2, further including a flowmeter to measure a flowrate of the cooling fluid flowing through the cooling baseplate. 5. The power inverter as recited in claim 1 , wherein the inverter circuit includes a temperature sensor and wherein the cooling capacity of the cooling baseplate is approximated as a function of temperature data from the inverter circuit temperature sensor. 6. The power inverter as recited in claim 3, wherein the cooling capacity of the cooling baseplate is calculated as a function of the temperature of the cooling fluid. 7. The power inverter as recited in claim 4, wherein the cooling capacity of the cooling baseplate is calculated as a function of the flowrate of the cooling fluid. 8. A method to dynamically determine and limit the maximal current available from a power inverter including electronic switches controlled by a controller and cooled by a cooling baseplate, the power inverter having a DC input connectable to a power source and an AC output connectable to a load, the method comprising: - characterizing the temperature of the electronic switches with respect to a cooling capacity of the cooling baseplate; - determining equations linking the temperature of the electronic switches with respect to the cooling capacity of the baseplate; - acquiring an instantaneous cooling capacity of the cooling baseplate, an instantaneous value of the voltage of the power source and an instantaneous value of a current supplied by the power inverter; - determinea dynamic value of the maximal current as a function of the acquired values; and - limiting the output current of the power inverter to the calculated dynamic value of the maximal current. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the instantaneous cooling capacity acquiring is done by acquiring at least one of a temperature and flow rate of a cooling fluid going through the cooling baseplate. 10. The method of claim 8, wherein the instantaneous cooling capacity acquiring is done by estimating a temperature of the cooling baseplate. 1 1 . The method of claim 10, wherein the temperature of the cooling baseplate is estimated by using the temperature read by temperature sensors provided in the power inverter. 12. The method of claim 10, wherein the equation determination is done by the construction of look-up tables. 13. A method to dynamically determine and limit the maximal current available from a power inverter including electronic switches controlled by a controller and cooled by a cooling baseplate, the power inverter having a DC input connectable to a power source and an AC output connectable to a load, the power inverter including a temperature sensor, the method comprising: - characterizing the temperature of the electronic switches with respect to a temperature data from the temperature sensor; - determining equations linking the temperature of the electronic switches with respect to the temperature data from the temperature sensor; - acquiring instantaneous temperature data from the temperature sensor, an instantaneous value of the voltage of the power source and an instantaneous value of a current supplied by the power inverter; - determining a dynamic value of the maximal current as a function of the acquired values; and - limiting the output current of the power inverter to the calculated dynamic value of the maximal current. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the characterizing is done by measuring the temperature of the switches and recording the 

 temperature from the temperature sensor for different operating conditions of the power inverter. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the different operating conditions of the power inverter include at least one of an input voltage, a switching frequency and an output current. 16. The method of claim 13, wherein the equation determination is done by the construction of look-up tables.
